req.title: Webhook delivery
req.statement: Outbound webhooks shall retry on 5xx with exponential backoff up to 5 times; permanent failures shall be DLQ'd.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for outbound webhook retry behavior with exponential backoff
and Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) handling.

Requirements:
- Retry on 5xx HTTP responses with exponential backoff
- Maximum 5 retry attempts
- Permanent failures (exhausted retries) must be sent to DLQ
"""

import time
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, Mock, call, patch

import httpx
import pytest
import respx

from webhook.dispatcher import WebhookDispatcher  # adjust import to your project structure
from webhook.models import WebhookEvent, WebhookDeliveryAttempt  # adjust as needed


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def sample_event():
    return WebhookEvent(
        id="evt_001",
        url="https://example.com/webhook",
        payload={"event": "order.created", "order_id": 42},
        headers={"X-Signature": "sha256=abc123"},
    )


@pytest.fixture
def dlq():
    """Fake Dead Letter Queue."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def dispatcher(dlq):
    return WebhookDispatcher(dlq=dlq, base_delay=0.01)  # fast backoff for tests


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ALL_5XX_CODES = [500, 502, 503, 504, 507, 508, 511]
NON_RETRYABLE_4XX = [400, 401, 403, 404, 410, 422, 429]
SUCCESS_CODES = [200, 201, 202, 204]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Successful delivery (no retry needed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestSuccessfulDelivery:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("status_code", SUCCESS_CODES)
    @respx.mock
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_delivers_successfully_on_2xx(self, dispatcher, sample_event, status_code):
        respx.post(sample_event.url).mock(return_value=httpx.Response(status_code))

        result = await dispatcher.send(sample_event)

        assert result.success is True
        assert result.attempts == 1
        assert result.status_code == status_code

    @respx.mock
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_no_retry_on_success(self, dispatcher, sample_event):
        route = respx.post(sample_event.url).mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200))

        await dispatcher.send(sample_event)

        assert route.call_count == 1

    @respx.mock
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_dlq_not_called_on_success(self, dispatcher, dlq, sample_event):
        respx.post(sample_event.url).mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200))

        await dispatcher.send(sample_event)

        dlq.send.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Retry on 5xx responses
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestRetryOn5xx:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("status_code", ALL_5XX_CODES)
    @respx.mock
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_retries_on_5xx_status(self, dispatcher, sample_event, status_code):
        """Any 5xx response triggers at least one retry."""
        route = respx.post(sample_event.url).mock(
            side_effect=[
                httpx.Response(status_code),
                httpx.Response(200),
            ]
        )

        result = await dispatcher.send(sample_event)

        assert route.call_count == 2
        assert result.success is True

    @respx.mock
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_succeeds_after_transient_5xx(self, dispatcher, sample_event):
        """Succeeds when a 5xx is followed by a 200 within retry budget."""
        route = respx.post(sample_event.url).mock(
            side_effect=[
                httpx.Response(503),
                httpx.Response(503),
                httpx.Response(200),
            ]
        )

        result = await dispatcher.send
